author: Sabir-ud-din, Muhammad, Maulvi
title: Al-Aqwal us Sabitah fi Karahat-il-Jamaat-i-Saniah
gloss: A controversial pamphlet in which the writer tries to prove that according to the Muhammadan law more than one prayer meetings cannot be held in one and the same mosque for one and the same prayer.
notes: Fiqh micro-dispute (second congregation in one mosque) — the granularity of the pamphlet wars.

author: Sewa Singh, Bhai
title: Paththar Puja Kiyun ki Jati hai
gloss: A tract condemning idol worship with quotations from the Puranas.
notes: Sikh anti-idolatry tract arguing from the Puranas themselves — cross-scriptural polemic technique; 4 pages, free.

author: Zahir-ud-din Muhammad, Munshi
title: Ved ke Zahur men Futur
gloss: A controversial pamphlet in which the writer tries to prove that the teachings of the Kuran are superior to those of the Vedas.
notes: Muslim–Arya front of the quarter's polemic ring ('Defects in the Manifestation of the Vedas'). Urdu—Religion closes at s103: 24 entries this quarter.

flags.issue: margin serial obscured by pencil X; read as 103 (follows 102)

author: Lachhman Singh, Sodhi
title: Amali Masahat
gloss: Or a book on mensuration for the use of the Primary classes, patwaris and qanungos.
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. Same Sodhi Lachhman Singh as the land-records booklet (p24 s167) — revenue-administration pedagogy as an authorial niche.

author: Muhammad Siraj-ud-din, Maulvi
title: Hisab ki Kitab
gloss: Arithmetic for the boys of the infant and lower Primary classes.
notes: Author registered from Rangoon — the Urdu textbook market's reach across the Bay of Bengal (with Baluchistan p21 s15, Bijnor p24 s169, Srinagar below: this quarter's out-of-province registrations map a print catchment far beyond Punjab).

title: Qaum-i-Turk
gloss: A translation by M. Muhammad Insha Ullah, Editor, the 'Watan,' Lahore, of Garnet's 'The Turkish People.'
copyright: M. Muhammad Insha Ullah, Lahore; No. 1—, dated the 2—th October, 1910.
notes: Third Insha-Ullah/Watan translation this quarter (with Jadid Turki Daur p28 s5 and Injil-i-Barnabas p30 s90) — Lucy Garnett's ethnography of the Turks completing his Ottoman trilogy; a one-editor pan-Islamic publishing program visible in a single catalog.

flags.issue: register No. and day partially cut at page edge

title: Aurad-i-Qadiriya
gloss: A collection of the prayers of the adherents of the order of the Qadiri Sufis, with translation in Persian.
notes: Qadiri litany with Persian translation, published by a Srinagar pir through the rival Mujaddidi order's Amritsar press — Kashmir joins the quarter's out-of-province registration map.
